description: §41.16.2 — the rows= attribute SHALL evaluate to an array of the resolved struct type (StructType[]). A quoted string-literal value (`rows="notAnArray"`) is a type mismatch — a string is not `User[]` — and fires E-TABLEFOR-ROWS-WRONG-TYPE (the sibling of E-TABLEFOR-ROWS-MISSING, which fires on absence). (Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 + W-STDLIB-SEED-FAILCLOSED co-fire, as in the sibling table-for cases; superset match.)
rationale: §41.16.2 E-TABLEFOR-ROWS-WRONG-TYPE: a present rows= value whose type is not StructType[] is a type mismatch. A quoted string-literal value is the structural-shape form the compiler rejects at the type-system stage. Fix: pass an array-of-struct expression, e.g. `rows=@users` or `rows=@items.filter(p)`.
